Vahga is mentioned by Kalidasa (Raghu. IV-36) as situated
in the delta formed by the Ganga and the Brahmaputra. According
to Pargiter it comprised the districts of Murshidabad, Nadia,
Jessore, parts of Rajshahi, Pabna and Faridpur.
Upavahga has been identified with the central area of the east-
ern part of the delta of the GaAga. According to Alberuni Jatha-
raAga consists of two territories viz. Jathara and Ahga.
The Sulikas are called Colikas in the Puranas. In the Matsya-
purana this country is said to be watered by the Caksu (Oxus). A
branch of the Sulikas that migrated to Orissa is to be identified
with the Sulkis. P.G. Bagchi connects the ^Qlikas with the Caluk-
yas of the Deccan.
Vidarbha comprised modern Berar and the territory between
the rivers Varada and WaingaAga. Acarya Daijdin praises the
Vaidarbhi style as containing all the ten literary excellences. In
ancient times it was a famous country where lived distinguished
scholars and poets.
